rules: Every direct dependency is pinned with an exact Cargo requirement and committed lockfile.
Every direct and transitive package requires an accepted license and deterministic-impact review.
Authoritative dependencies disable default features and enable only reviewed features.
A version or feature change is a new review and must rerun affected cross-platform goldens.
Unreviewed registry, Git, binary-download and dynamic-plugin dependencies fail repository checks.

compile_cost_measurement.runner: Windows x86_64-pc-windows-msvc; rustc 1.97.0; local development measurement (not a stable performance runner)
compile_cost_measurement.scope: starclock-combat plus fixnum and all three transitive packages
compile_cost_measurement.classification: Low baseline; remeasure after dependency changes

production_reader_compile_cost_measureme.runner: Windows x86_64-pc-windows-msvc; rustc 1.97.0; local development measurement (not a stable performance runner)
production_reader_compile_cost_measureme.scope: starclock-data, generated Sora 0.3.0 reader, serde/zstd graph and all local dependencies
production_reader_compile_cost_measureme.classification: Moderate generated-reader/toolchain cost; no runtime performance claim

rng_hash_compile_cost_measurement.runner: Windows x86_64-pc-windows-msvc; rustc 1.97.0; local development measurement (not a stable performance runner)
rng_hash_compile_cost_measurement.scope: starclock-combat plus fixnum, rand/ChaCha8, SHA-256 and all direct/transitive packages
rng_hash_compile_cost_measurement.classification: Low local compile cost; replay compatibility is governed by golden vectors, not this timing

replay_codec_compile_cost_measurement.runner: Windows x86_64-pc-windows-msvc; rustc 1.97.0; local development measurement (not a stable performance runner)
replay_codec_compile_cost_measurement.scope: starclock-replay, activity/combat dependencies and the already reviewed numeric/RNG/SHA-256 graph
replay_codec_compile_cost_measurement.classification: Low local compile cost; canonical compatibility is governed by codec goldens

property_harness_compile_cost_measuremen.runner: Windows x86_64-pc-windows-msvc; rustc 1.97.0; local development measurement (not a stable performance runner)
property_harness_compile_cost_measuremen.command: fresh CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test --no-run -p starclock-combat -p starclock-replay --all-features --locked
property_harness_compile_cost_measuremen.scope: starclock-combat and starclock-replay test targets plus the reviewed proptest 1.11.0 dev-only graph
property_harness_compile_cost_measuremen.classification: Moderate test-only compile cost; no production binary, runtime or performance impact

benchmark_harness_compile_cost_measureme.runner: Windows x86_64-pc-windows-msvc; rustc 1.97.0; local development measurement (not the strict stable performance report)
benchmark_harness_compile_cost_measureme.command: fresh CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo build -p starclock-cli --example g01_benchmark --features benchmark-harness --release --locked
benchmark_harness_compile_cost_measureme.scope: Benchmark-only example plus allocation-counter 0.8.1 and existing workspace graph
benchmark_harness_compile_cost_measureme.classification: Moderate benchmark-only release-build cost; allocation-counter adds one dependency and no production edge

packages.name: allocation-counter
packages.purpose: Measure per-thread allocation count, total bytes and peak live bytes for versioned battle and replay workloads without adding unsafe allocator code to Starclock.
packages.deterministic_impact: Benchmark instrumentation only behind the benchmark-harness feature; it does not enter authoritative state, production CLI behavior, replay bytes or simulation dependency edges.
packages.compile_cost: Part of a 21,720 ms fresh locked release example build including the existing workspace graph.
rejected_alternatives: Handwritten global allocator wrapper: would introduce project-owned unsafe code solely for measurement.
Process RSS sampling: cannot isolate allocations per command or per concurrent job.
Estimated Vec capacities alone: does not measure actual allocator traffic or peak live bytes.

packages.name: fixnum
packages.purpose: Checked decimal fixed-point storage and exact add/subtract/integer operations behind Starclock domain wrappers; the project-owned promoted quotient implements the complete six-mode rounding contract.
packages.deterministic_impact: Authoritative numeric backend; exact version, features, raw six-decimal representation, project rounding policy and golden vectors are replay-sensitive.
packages.compile_cost: Part of the measured 1161 ms fresh combat check; four registry packages total.
rejected_alternatives: Handwritten wide fixed-point arithmetic: larger audit surface for overflow and rounding.
rust_decimal or floating point: broader representation/API surface and unsuitable authoritative float paths.

packages.name: itoa
packages.purpose: Integer-to-text support selected transitively by fixnum.
packages.deterministic_impact: Not used for canonical state encoding; remains locked because formatted numeric diagnostics must not float by dependency resolution.
packages.compile_cost: Part of the measured 1161 ms fresh combat check.
rejected_alternatives: Not independently replaceable without replacing or patching the reviewed fixnum package.

packages.name: static_assertions
packages.purpose: Compile-time type/layout assertions selected transitively by fixnum.
packages.deterministic_impact: Compile-time only and absent from authoritative state; exact resolution is still locked.
packages.compile_cost: Part of the measured 1161 ms fresh combat check.
rejected_alternatives: Not independently replaceable without replacing or patching the reviewed fixnum package.

packages.name: typenum
packages.purpose: Type-level decimal precision used by fixnum's six-place representation.
packages.deterministic_impact: Selects the compile-time representation; never appears in Starclock's public API or canonical bytes.
packages.compile_cost: Part of the measured 1161 ms fresh combat check.
rejected_alternatives: Not independently replaceable without replacing or patching the reviewed fixnum package.

package_groups.name: authoritative RNG and SHA-256
package_groups.relationship: rand and sha2 are direct starclock-combat dependencies; sha2 is also a direct starclock-build, starclock-activity, starclock-data, starclock-mode-universe and starclock-replay dependency; all other entries are transitive through those packages
package_groups.owner: starclock-combat private ChaCha8/stream-derivation implementation, starclock-build private canonical definition/build encoder, starclock-activity private RNG/participant/result/state/transaction encoders, starclock-data and starclock-mode-universe private exact bundle/catalog identity encoders, and starclock-replay private canonical SHA-256 sink
package_groups.purpose: Provide the explicitly named ChaCha8 core and SHA-256 primitive while Starclock owns canonical RNG stream bytes, build definition/catalog/selection encoding, activity handoff/result/state encoding, exact input-bundle hashing, unsigned rejection mapping, cumulative weight selection, draw consumption, counters and replay/state encoding.
package_groups.deterministic_impact: Authoritative and replay-sensitive. Exact versions/features, raw u64 words, battle/activity seed derivation bytes, build/activity digest domains, mapping and draw policy are bound by revisioned encoders and golden vectors; thread/sys RNG features are disabled and generic distribution APIs are mechanically unused.
package_groups.compile_cost: A fresh locked starclock-combat check including numeric, RNG and SHA-256 packages measured 3,500 ms on the recorded Windows runner. Existing shared cfg-if, libc and typenum packages are inventoried elsewhere.
rejected_alternatives: Thread/system RNG or generic rand distributions: hidden entropy and version-sensitive mapping/draw counts.
Handwritten ChaCha8 or SHA-256: unnecessary cryptographic primitive audit surface.
rand_chacha as a separate direct dependency: rand 0.10.2 exposes the required named ChaCha8 generator behind its reviewed chacha feature.

package_groups.name: property-test harness
package_groups.relationship: proptest is a direct dev-dependency of starclock-combat and starclock-replay; all other entries are transitive test-only packages
package_groups.owner: fixed-seed arithmetic, RNG mapping, catalog ordering, canonical codec and malformed replay property suites
package_groups.purpose: Generate and shrink bounded legal and malformed cases while persisting reproducible regression seeds beside the owning test crate.
package_groups.deterministic_impact: Test generation only. Every committed suite fixes the Proptest ChaCha seed, case count and shrink limit; generated values never enter production configuration, authoritative RNG, replay bytes or runtime packages.
package_groups.compile_cost: A fresh locked no-run build of combat and replay test targets measured 12,266 ms on the recorded Windows runner; the temporary 297.2 MiB target directory was removed after measurement.
rejected_alternatives: Handwritten pseudo-property loops: no structured shrinking or persisted minimal regressions.
QuickCheck: less explicit strategy composition and failure-persistence control for the planned malformed structured inputs.
Default Proptest features: add unneeded fork/timeout subprocess machinery and bit-set support.

packages.name: wasip2
packages.license: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception OR Apache-2.0 OR MIT

packages.name: wit-bindgen
packages.license: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception OR Apache-2.0 OR MIT

package_groups.name: production Sora generated reader
package_groups.relationship: serde and zstd are direct starclock-data and starclock-mode-universe dependencies; serde_json is a direct starclock-mode-universe dependency for bounded embedded score-curve, Occurrence-vector and rule-parameter fields; all other entries are transitive through those packages
package_groups.owner: starclock-data and starclock-mode-universe private generated transport readers plus three starclock-mode-universe private embedded-field lowering modules
package_groups.purpose: Compile and execute pinned Sora 0.3.0 generated readers for the core and isolated Standard Universe production bundles, then parse schema-authored bounded score-curve, Occurrence-vector and rule-parameter cells into typed domain values.
package_groups.deterministic_impact: Transport and three deny-unknown-fields embedded value decoders only; exact versions remain locked and generated/JSON transport values are converted before crossing a Starclock domain API.
package_groups.compile_cost: The prior fresh starclock-data check measured 18,782 ms; G04-P1-B1 reuses the identical locked Serde/Zstd graph in a second private generated reader and adds no registry package.
rejected_alternatives: Patching generated code to remove serde/zstd would fork pinned Sora output.
Diagnostic JSON or handwritten binary decoding would create a forbidden competing runtime path.

package_groups.name: frozen MCP adapter and bounded HTTP transport
package_groups.relationship: rmcp, schemars, serde_json, tokio and axum are direct starclock-mcp dependencies; tower and allocation-counter are test/benchmark-only; all other entries are transitive or target-specific through the exact locked adapter graph
package_groups.owner: starclock-mcp frozen 2025-11-25 stdio and Streamable HTTP adapter
package_groups.purpose: Provide the frozen official MCP protocol implementation, generated JSON Schemas and bounded Axum/Tokio Streamable HTTP integration while Starclock retains authorization, ownership, quotas, rates and domain semantics.
package_groups.deterministic_impact: Adapter and operational transport only. Exact versions, checksums, features and protocol fixtures are frozen by policy/mcp-sdk-lock.json; no package enters authoritative combat state, replay, RNG or hashes.
package_groups.compile_cost: Covered by locked starclock-mcp and capability-fixture builds on the pinned Windows/Rust runner; transport compile cost is operational and makes no simulation performance claim.
rejected_alternatives: Handwritten MCP JSON-RPC and Streamable HTTP: duplicates a frozen official protocol surface and expands the security audit boundary.
Floating SDK or default features: permits protocol, transport and dependency drift.
Embedding HTTP or MCP dependencies below starclock-mcp: violates the one-way protocol-neutral agent boundary.

packages.name: wasi
packages.license: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception OR Apache-2.0 OR MIT

tools.name: rustc
tools.purpose: Pinned compiler for all workspace targets.
tools.deterministic_impact: Compiler and standard-library changes can alter diagnostics, code generation and platform behavior; cross-platform golden bytes remain the authority.
tools.compile_cost: Toolchain baseline; no additional crate compile unit.
rejected_alternatives: Floating stable channel: non-reproducible CI and generated diagnostics.
Nightly: unnecessary unstable surface.

tools.name: cargo
tools.purpose: Workspace resolution, lockfile and build orchestration.
tools.deterministic_impact: Resolver v3 and Cargo.lock define the exact package graph.
tools.compile_cost: Build orchestrator; measurement reported per command instead.
rejected_alternatives: Unpinned Cargo: resolver and metadata drift.

tools.name: rustfmt
tools.deterministic_impact: Source formatting only; version drift would cause generated review noise.
rejected_alternatives: Editor-specific formatting: inconsistent checked source.

tools.name: clippy
tools.purpose: Pinned Rust lint gate with warnings denied.
tools.deterministic_impact: Static analysis only; pin avoids lint-set drift between local and CI gates.
tools.compile_cost: Prebuilt toolchain component; adds analysis time but no runtime code.
rejected_alternatives: Compiler warnings alone: insufficient deterministic/idiomatic checks.

tools.name: node
tools.purpose: Deterministic repository evidence, manifest and architecture verification scripts.
tools.deterministic_impact: Tooling only; scripts sort inputs and hash explicit UTF-8 bytes. Exact runtime is pinned by .node-version.
tools.compile_cost: Prebuilt external tool; no Rust compile unit.
rejected_alternatives: Deno/Bun: no existing repository baseline.
Immediate Rust rewrites: unnecessary bootstrap compile cost before the CLI/tool crates exist.

tools.name: sora-cli
tools.purpose: The sole schema validation, Excel projection/synchronization, Rust code generation and production bundle export tool.
tools.deterministic_impact: Schema semantics and generated/runtime formats are version-sensitive; the crates.io archive SHA-256, upstream tag identities, command syntax and stable output bytes are committed in the Sora capability lock.
tools.compile_cost: A locked local Windows x86_64 MSVC installation under Rust 1.97.0 took 102,500 ms; the tool and its dependency graph remain outside the production workspace.
rejected_alternatives: Floating Sora releases: schema and generated output drift.
JSON-direct runtime or handwritten spreadsheet readers: violate the selected Excel/Sora authority chain.
A source-build Git checkout: larger mutable trust surface than the checksum-bound crates.io release archive.
